wsgiref.handlers/BaseHandler/wsgi_file_w.name: wsgi_file_wrapper
wsgiref.handlers/BaseHandler/wsgi_file_w.description: A wsgi.file_wrapper factory, compatible with wsgiref.types.FileWrapper, or None. The default value of this attribute is the wsgiref.util.FileWrapper class.

info.excerpt: A wsgi.file_wrapper factory, compatible with wsgiref.types.FileWrapper, or None. The default value of this attribute is the wsgiref.util.FileWrapper class.

wsgiref.handlers/BaseHandler/wsgi_multip.name: wsgi_multiprocess
wsgiref.handlers/BaseHandler/wsgi_multip.description: The value to be used for the wsgi.multiprocess environment variable. It defaults to true in BaseHandler, but may have a different default (or be set by the constructor) in the other subclasses.

info.excerpt: The value to be used for the wsgi.multiprocess environment variable. It defaults to true in BaseHandler, but may have a different default (or be set by the constructor) in the other subclasses.

wsgiref.handlers/BaseHandler/wsgi_multit.name: wsgi_multithread
wsgiref.handlers/BaseHandler/wsgi_multit.description: The value to be used for the wsgi.multithread environment variable. It defaults to true in BaseHandler, but may have a different default (or be set by the constructor) in the other subclasses.

info.excerpt: The value to be used for the wsgi.multithread environment variable. It defaults to true in BaseHandler, but may have a different default (or be set by the constructor) in the other subclasses.

wsgiref.handlers/BaseHandler/wsgi_run_on.name: wsgi_run_once
wsgiref.handlers/BaseHandler/wsgi_run_on.description: The value to be used for the wsgi.run_once environment variable. It defaults to false in BaseHandler, but CGIHandler sets it to true by default.

info.excerpt: The value to be used for the wsgi.run_once environment variable. It defaults to false in BaseHandler, but CGIHandler sets it to true by default.

wsgiref.handlers/WSGIRequestHandler.name: WSGIRequestHandler
wsgiref.handlers/WSGIRequestHandler.description: Create an HTTP handler for the given request (i.e. a socket), client_address (a (host,port) tuple), and server (WSGIServer instance). You do not need to create instances of this class directly; they are automatically created as needed by WSGIServer objects. You can, however, subclass this class and supply it as a handler_class to the make_server() function. wsgiref.handlers/WSGIServer.name: WSGIServer
wsgiref.handlers/WSGIServer.description: Create a WSGIServer instance. server_address should be a (host,port) tuple, and RequestHandlerClass should be the subclass of http.server.BaseHTTPRequestHandler that will be used to process requests. You do not normally need to call this constructor, as the make_server() function can handle all the details for you. WSGIServer is a subclass of http.server.HTTPServer, so all of its methods (such as serve_forever() and handle_request()) are available. WSGIServer also provides these WSGI-specific...

wsgiref.handlers/types/WSGIApplication.description: A type alias describing a WSGI application callable.

wsgiref.handlers/types/WSGIEnvironment.name: WSGIEnvironment
wsgiref.handlers/types/WSGIEnvironment.description: A type alias describing a WSGI environment dictionary.
